abstraction_fitness.description: The lib/ layer contains 12 modules with real domain logic — no pass-through wrappers, no delegation-heavy classes, and no single-implementation interface ecosystems. Every module earns its indirection.

abstraction_fitness.header: Unsafe cast in search.ts groupRestaurantsByName re-export
abstraction_fitness.description: lib/search.ts re-exports groupRestaurantsByName from lib/restaurant-grouping.ts but uses 'as unknown as GroupableRestaurant[]' to bridge a structural type mismatch between RestaurantWithRecommendations and GroupableRestaurant. This is a persistent open issue.
isValidTransition and validNextStates are exported from lib/state-machine.ts but have no production callers — only test file imports. These add API surface without substitution value.

dependency_health.description: Only four runtime dependencies: next@14.1.3, react@^18.2.0, react-dom@^18.2.0, @prisma/client@^6.19.2. No overlapping libraries, no heavy deps for light use, no circular cycles detectable.

dependency_health.header: next version pinned to exact minor
dependency_health.description: next is pinned to 14.1.3 (a specific minor version) while other deps use caret ranges. This inconsistency is minor and does not cause material risk.

low_level_elegance.header: Clean control flow in restaurant-grouping.ts and state-machine.ts
low_level_elegance.description: lib/restaurant-grouping.ts and lib/state-machine.ts have direct, intention-revealing control flow with well-bounded mutation. These modules serve as good examples of local craft in the codebase.
The validateRedditPost function uses a field array loop while all other seven validators use manual null/empty check pairs. This persistent inconsistency doubles code volume for field validation.
searchRestaurants and countSearchResults both build identical Prisma where-condition logic (~35-40 LOC) in separate function bodies. Any filter change requires editing both.

low_level_elegance.header: scrape/route.ts calls parseQuery twice for same input
low_level_elegance.description: The POST handler calls parseQuery(query) once inside fetchWithFallback (line 24) and once directly (line 194) for the same query string, doubling LLM API usage and latency per scrape.

mid_level_elegance.header: Inconsistent grouping responsibility across search boundary
mid_level_elegance.description: The initial search API groups results server-side; the paginated search API returns raw ungrouped restaurants requiring the client component to re-apply grouping logic. The boundary contract differs between the two sibling endpoints.

mid_level_elegance.header: fetchWithFallback advance() callback creates hidden shared state
mid_level_elegance.description: The function mutates caller-owned state through a passed callback rather than returning its computed state transitions, creating an opaque shared-state pattern across a function boundary.

mid_level_elegance.header: Background scrape trigger has no rate-limiting or deduplication
mid_level_elegance.description: triggerScrape in /api/search fires unconditionally on every low-result request with no guard against concurrent identical scrapes for the same city+query.

test_strategy.description: lib/hours.ts, lib/restaurant-grouping.ts (normalizeWebsite, groupRestaurantsByName), and lib/openrouter.ts (regexFallback, parseQueryWithLLM, getRedditRecommendations) all now have direct test coverage. These were flagged in prior reviews as untested critical paths.
All major API routes have dedicated test files: api-search.test.ts, api-search-more.test.ts, api-votes.test.ts, api-geo.test.ts, api-trending.test.ts. These cover happy paths and key error cases.

test_strategy.header: lib/rules.ts tests mock Prisma — DB query logic unexercised
test_strategy.description: Rules tests validate mock call shape only. The actual Prisma where clauses in checkRule01, checkRule03, checkRule05 are never executed against real database behavior, leaving query correctness untested.

isValidRestaurantName, isPostRelevantToQuery, and isRecommendationRequest are pure filter functions that gate all restaurant extraction but have no dedicated tests. Only extractRestaurantName is covered.

context_updates.api_surface_coherence: Both previously-flagged issues confirmed open. Mixed sync/async exports: lib/geo.ts (detectCityFromIp async + extractIp sync), lib/search.ts (4 async + groupRestaurantsByName sync), lib/reddit.ts (2 async + extractRestaurantName sync). Envelope divergence: /api/search returns grouped RestaurantGroup[], /api/search-more returns ungrouped RestaurantWithRecommendations[], forcing LoadMoreButton to re-run groupRestaurantsByName client-side.
context_updates.authorization_consistency: Previously-flagged issue confirmed open: /api/geo returns raw IP in debug.ip field and full Nominatim address object in debug.address field on every response, ungated by environment. New finding: INTERNAL_API_TOKEN check on /api/scrape is conditional — absent env var means endpoint is fully open to the public.
context_updates.ai_generated_debt: Previously-flagged issue confirmed open: lib/validate.ts repeats null/undefined + empty-string check pairs ~20 times across 9 functions with a loop/manual inconsistency. Additional finding: validateExtractedRestaurant in lib/reddit.ts is called inside two catch-all try-catch blocks that swallow ValidationError with a console.warn, voiding the validation contract entirely.
context_updates.incomplete_migration: Dimension clean. No TODO/FIXME/DEPRECATED markers, no JS/TS mixed files, no old library coexistence, no stale migration shims found in any .ts/.tsx source file.
context_updates.package_organization: Previously-flagged issue confirmed open: lib/ flat with 12 files, 3 identifiable concern groups (external API clients, domain logic, infrastructure). state-machine.ts and google-places.ts are low-discovery stragglers with only 1 importer each. No new placement problems found.

add.header: Prisma singleton follows canonical Next.js pattern
add.description: lib/db.ts uses globalThis-based singleton to survive dev hot-reload. This is the recommended Next.js pattern and not an initialization coupling defect.

add.header: reddit.ts captures env vars at module scope
add.description: CLIENT_ID and CLIENT_SECRET are read into module-scope constants at import time, making them stale in tests that configure process.env after import. A module-level mutable OAuth token cache compounds the risk for concurrent requests.

add.header: scrape/route.ts calls parseQuery twice per request
add.description: The POST handler calls parseQuery inside fetchWithFallback and again directly in the handler body — two LLM API calls for the same input on every scrape request.

add.header: Status badge map duplicated across two components
add.description: STATUS_BADGE (TrendingSection.tsx) and STATUS_LABELS (RestaurantCard.tsx) define identical VERIFIED/UNREVIEWED/INCOMPLETE → label+classname mappings. Any badge change must be applied in two places.

add.header: app/api/scrape/route.ts is an active Next.js route entry point
add.description: The orphaned signal is a false positive — Next.js routes have zero static importers by design. The file is live production code triggered via HTTP POST.

add.header: Validator functions have consistent throw contracts
add.description: All 9 validator functions in lib/validate.ts throw ValidationError on invalid input and return void on success — a clean, uniform contract across the module.

Both vote route handlers call tryTransitionToVerified after committing a Prisma transaction. If the transition Prisma call throws, the route returns HTTP 500 even though the vote was successfully persisted.
When city detection returns null, TrendingSection defaults to displaying 'Ottawa' — a misleading city label for non-Ottawa users with failed geolocation.

add.header: Guard clause pattern used consistently in lib/
add.description: All lib/ async functions use early-return guard clauses on missing env vars and invalid inputs before proceeding to I/O. This makes the happy path easy to trace.

add.header: External API boundaries use appropriate loose typing
add.description: lib/google-places.ts and lib/openrouter.ts correctly use Record<string, unknown> for untyped external JSON responses and add casts only after explicit shape checks.

add.header: VoteDirection enum bypassed with raw string comparison in both vote routes
add.description: Both vote handlers validate direction as 'up'|'down' strings and then map to VoteDirection manually. The enum is available but bypassed, weakening the type-narrowing contract.
